title: Mid-Line Disk Storage Emerging As Significant Cost-Saving Opportunity
author: Aberdeen Group
license: Aberdeen Group's Executive White Paper presenting the case for a new 'mid-line' disk storage tier using ATA technology positioned between high-performance FC/SCSI disk arrays and tape. Based on 75 face-to-face and telephone interviews with Fortune-class storage managers. The paper defines four tiers of the storage pyramid (high-performance disk / mid-line / near-line / tape) and argues that mid-line ATA disk can serve at least 20% of enterprise data — and potentially over half — at 50% lower cost per gigabyte. It also introduces information lifecycle management (ILM) as the strategic framework for multi-tier storage.
relevance: The published deliverable of the Aberdeen-Maxtor SOW engagement — the formal launch artifact of the midline storage category. Aberdeen's first major published articulation of the mid-line tier concept backed by 75 enterprise interviews. This white paper is the intellectual foundation of the broader Pools of Storage program.
prescience: ILM tiered storage and the concept of aligning data to cost-appropriate storage based on access patterns remain current enterprise architecture principles. The white paper's four ILM principles (Aging/Freezing/Accumulation/Redundancy) are still analytically valid. ATA-based midline storage is now the standard (SATA SSDs and HDDs dominate secondary tiers).
column17: Predicted: (1) mid-line ATA would become a distinct enterprise storage tier — confirmed; (2) 75% of storage buyers moderately or highly likely to buy mid-line in the coming year — substantially confirmed by SATA HDD adoption curves 2004-2008; (3) mid-line would reduce cost of 2TB backup appliance from $15-20K to $7-10K — confirmed; (4) IT organizations would need to rethink storage infrastructure around ILM — confirmed; (5) more than half of enterprise data would eventually fall into mid-line tier — substantially confirmed.
